journal_entry.reason: Factory electricity is a manufacturing overhead (product cost) capitalized to inventory; office electricity is a period expense. Corporate credit card payment creates a payable liability.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Mondelez incurred two types of electricity charges. The factory electricity of $15,000 is a manufacturing overhead cost that forms part of the cost of inventory produced (product cost under IAS 2), so it should be recorded as Manufacturing overhead or similar account that will be capitalized to inventory. The office electricity of $5,500 is a period expense recorded as Utilities expense. Both charges totaling $20,500 were paid using the corporate credit card, which creates a liability (credit card payable) rather than reducing cash. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax treatment is applied.
